title: Moral Responsibility / 道德责任

title: Discourse on Nikuda Brahmins / 佛說尼拘陀梵志經
summary: A Buddhist sutra depicting Buddha's dialogue with Nikuda brahmins, critiquing external ascetic practices and revealing defilement accumulation.

title: Nine Unnatural/Unexplained Deaths / 九橫 / 九横
summary: Nine categories of untimely deaths caused by preventable causes such as improper eating, wrong conduct, and association with evil companions.

title: 滅盡定 Nirodhāsamāpatti / Absorption of Cessation
summary: A Buddhist meditation attainment where all mental formations cease, enabling practitioners to grant present-world blessings to beings.

title: The Discourse on Niḡrodha Brahmā / 佛說尼拘陀梵志經
summary: A Mahāyāna sutra depicting the Buddha's discourse with the ascetic Niḡrodha, critiquing false ascetic practices and demonstrating the nature of true Buddhist practice.

title: Ollama Framework / Ollama 框架 / Ollamaフレームワーク
summary: An open-source runtime environment for running large language models locally with quantized GGUF model files.

title: Political Philosophy in Analects / 论语政治哲学
summary: Confucian governance principles including meritocracy, moral leadership, and social stability

title: Providing the People with Sustenance (制民之產) / 民に産業を制する
summary: Mencius argued that benevolent government requires establishing the people's material welfare first—providing land, livestock, and education—because only those with stable livelihoods can develop constant virtue, making economic security the foundation of moral governance.

title: English Title / 中文标题
summary: One-line summary in English

title: Ren (Benevolence) / 仁
summary: Central Confucian virtue of humaneness and perfect virtue

title: Internal and External Righteousness / 仁內義外 / 仁内義外
summary: The classical Confucian debate about whether righteousness (義) originates from within or is determined by external circumstances

title: The Debate on Rites versus Food / 礼与食孰重
summary: The philosophical debate in Mencius about whether ceremonial rites (li) should take precedence over practical necessities like food and marriage, exploring the tension between moral principles and human needs.

title: 三種觀 / Threefold Contemplation Practice
summary: The three meditation methods of empty view, phenomenal view, and middle way observation central to Mahayana Buddhist practice.

title: Three Dharmas Classification System (三法)
summary: A Buddhist doctrinal classification system organizing teachings into virtue, evil, and liberation paths.

title: Three Dharmas Treatise (三法度論)
summary: A Buddhist philosophical treatise organized into three sections explaining virtue, evil, and liberation paths.

title: Seven Buddhas of the Current Kalpa / 七佛
summary: The seven Buddhas who have appeared in the current kalpa, from Vipassin to Shakyamuni.

title: Seven Factors of Awakening / 七覺意
summary: The Seven Factors of Awakening (念、择法、精进、喜、轻安、定、捨) as explained in An Shigao's Han Dynasty Buddhist commentary.

title: Shared Joy with the People (與民偕樂) / 民と偕に楽む
summary: Mencius's principle that rulers should share enjoyment with the people, not hoard pleasures for themselves.

title: 十門通判 / Ten-Fold Framework for Sutra Exposition
summary: A systematic ten-category methodology for analyzing and expounding Buddhist sutras, originating with Xianshou Fazang and elaborated by later masters.

title: 四恩 / Four Kindnesses / 四恩（しおりん）
summary: The Buddhist teaching on four debts of gratitude—to parents, teachers, sovereign, and sentient beings—that form the foundation of Buddhist ethical practice.

title: Tang Wan Kung / 唐宓公
summary: Overview of the historical and philosophical significance of Tang Wan Kung in Confucianism.

title: Eight Rare Qualities of a Tathagata / 如來八希有法
summary: Eight exceptional qualities that appear when a Buddha enters the world, as taught by Indra and repeated by Brahma, forming a unique doctrinal teaching of this sutra.

title: Teaching Has Many Methods / 教亦多术
summary: Mencius's pedagogical philosophy that不屑之教诲 (disdainful teaching) is itself a valid and effective method of moral education, representing a sophisticated understanding of educational psychology.

title: Ten Remembrances / 十念
summary: A Buddhist meditation practice involving ten objects of mindfulness, from the Commentary on Classifying Merit.

title: English Title / 中文标题
summary: One-line summary in English

title: Thirty-Seven Bodhiparamitas / 三十七品道品 / 三十七波羅蜜
summary: A detailed analysis of the thirty-seven practices leading to enlightenment as expounded in An Shigao's commentary on the Yin Chi Ru Jing.

title: Thirty-Seven Factors of Enlightenment / 三十七道品
summary: A systematic classification of Buddhist practice teachings organized in progressive numerical categories (1-10), as presented in An Shigao's translation of the Long Discourses.

title: Trayastriṃśa Heaven / 三十三天（忉利天）
summary: The Heaven of Thirty-Three Gods where the initial discourse of the Great Firm Brāhmaṇa Sūtra takes place, presided over by Śakra (帝釋天主) and the site of the first teaching of the eight rare qualities.

title: Three Dharmas / 三法
summary: A fundamental Buddhist philosophical framework comprising merit, evil, and liberation through dependent awakening.

title: Three Dharmas / 三法 / 三法
summary: A Buddhist doctrinal framework classifying all phenomena into three categories: virtue (德), evil (惡), and basis (依), each containing three subcategories.

title: Three Evil Roots / 三惡本 / 三悪根本
summary: The three fundamental sources of unwholesome karma: greed (贪), hatred (恚), and delusion (痴), from which all other evils arise.

title: Three Roots of Unwholesomeness / 三不善根
summary: The three fundamental unwholesome mental roots—greed, hatred, and delusion—that underlie all negative karmic actions in Buddhism.

title: 三藏三分 Four Āgamas / Tripartite Canon and Quadruple Sūtra Collections
summary: The organization of the Buddhist canon into three treasures and four sūtra collections, attributed to Ānanda's compilation guided by celestial beings.

title: Three Years Mourning / 三年之丧
summary: The traditional mourning period of three years emphasizes filial piety and respect for deceased parents.

title: Tian Shi Bu Ru Di Li, Di Li Bu Ru Ren He / 天時不如地利，地利不如人和
summary: The Mencian doctrine that favorable timing is less important than geographical advantage, which is less important than human harmony.

title: Three Universally Honored Things / 天下有三尊
summary: Mencius's doctrine that there are three things universally honored in the world: rank (position), age (seniority), and virtue (moral character), with implications for the hierarchy of honor in governance and society.

title: 頭陀行 / Dhutanga Practices
summary: 頭陀行乃佛教十三種清淨苦行，修習此法可去衣、食、住之貪著，速得解脫

title: Twelve Links of Dependent Origination / 十二因緣 / 十二縁起
summary: Analysis of the twelve links of dependent origination as explained in An Shigao's commentary on the Yin Chi Ru Jing.

title: Twelve Links of Dependent Origination / 十二因緣
summary: The Buddhist doctrine explaining the cyclical chain of causation from ignorance to suffering.

title: Unsummoned Ministers / 不召之臣
summary: A Confucian doctrine that great ministers of virtue possess inherent dignity that prevents them from being summoned by rulers like ordinary officials.

title: Expedient Means / 方便 / 方便
summary: Buddhist concept of upaya (expedient means) as the practical methods and path structures for achieving spiritual liberation.

title: Virtue and Ethics / 德与伦理
summary: Discusses the importance of virtue in Confucian ethics and its practical applications in governance and personal conduct.

title: Merit and Virtue in Buddhist Practice / 德 / 徳
summary: Buddhist concept of merit (德, puṇya) encompassing giving, morality, meditation, and the three roots of wholesome qualities.

title: Virtue Section (德品) - Buddhist Spiritual Cultivation
summary: The first section of the Three Dharmas Treatise detailing the cultivation of merit, roots of goodness, and non-evil qualities.

title: The Way of the True King vs. Hegemony (王道與霸道) / 王道と霸道
summary: Mencius's distinction between righteous rule through virtue and coercive rule through force.

title: White Cloth and Golden Banner Two Brahmins Origin Sutra / 白衣金幢二婆羅門緣起經
summary: A Buddhist sutra recounting the renunciation of two Brahmins and presenting a Buddhist cosmology of human social origins.

title: Willow-Cup Metaphor / 杞柳桮棬
summary: Gaozi's philosophical metaphor comparing human nature to willow wood that must be coerced into artificial forms, critiqued by Mencius.

title: 賢首五教 / Five Teachings of Xianshou / 賢首五教（けんじゅぎょう）
summary: A five-tiered doctrinal classification system developed by Fazang (Xianshou) of the Huayan school, categorizing all Buddhist teachings from Hinayana to the highest Yogacara-Tathagatagarbha doctrines.

title: 序分 / Buddhist Prologue Section
summary: The introductory section of Buddhist sutras establishing credibility and context for the main teachings.

title: Will and Passion-Nature / 志與情的關係
summary: Mencius's doctrine on the hierarchical yet interactive relationship between will (zhi) and passion-nature (qing)

title: Satori / 悟
summary: Satori is a Japanese Buddhist term for a sudden awakening or enlightenment experience, often associated with Zen practice.

summary: One-line summary in English

title: English Title / 中文标题
summary: One-line summary in English

title: Benevolent Governance / 仁政
summary: The Confucian ideal of humane government that seeks the well‑being of the people through moral leadership and virtuous policies.

title: Self-Cultivation / 修身
summary: The Confucian practice of refining one's moral character as the foundation for personal, familial, and societal harmony.

title: Confucianism / 儒家
summary: Confucianism is an ethical and philosophical system originating in ancient China, emphasizing moral virtue, proper conduct, and harmonious social relationships.

title: Junzi (君子) / 君子
summary: The Confucian ideal of the morally cultivated person, embodying virtue, righteousness, and social responsibility.
